title: PayPal Q1 2026 Earnings Beat: Strong Revenue and Payment Volume Growth Under New CEO
text: PayPal exceeded analyst expectations in its first earnings report under new CEO Enrique Lores. The company reported first-quarter revenue of $8.353 billion, representing 7% year-over-year growth, and adjusted earnings per share of $1.34, up 1% annually. Wall Street had anticipated earnings of $1.27 per share and revenue of $8.05 billion. Payment volume from merchant customers reached $463.95 billion, an 11% increase surpassing the consensus projection of $446.8 billion. Active accounts reached 439 million, up 1% year-over-year. Transaction margin dollars rose 3% to $3.810 billion. PayPal repurchased approximately 34 million shares for $1.5 billion. The company is implementing a cost reduction program targeting $1.5 billion over two to three years. Despite beating expectations, the stock gave back early gains and was trading down about 10% shortly after markets opened. PayPal projects a high-single-digit decline in non-GAAP EPS for Q2 2026 and reiterated full-year guidance calling for a low-single-digit decline to slightly positive results.
